title: Design System: Eric Mah — Personal Site

display.purpose: Hero name only. Compressed, massive, intentionally oversized at large viewports.

headline.purpose: Post page titles. Not used on the homepage.

label.purpose: Section labels exclusively. One per section, never in card bodies.

shadows.name: glass-ambient
shadows.purpose: Applied to all .glass surfaces. Part of the material, not a structural drop-shadow.

shadows.name: card-hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Atmospheric red bleed on interactive glass elements at hover state.

shadows.name: timeline-spine
shadows.purpose: The 2px red vertical line behind timeline nodes. Low-diffusion focus point.

shadows.name: amber-dot-glow
shadows.purpose: Amber sonar pulse on the ongoing timeline dot only. Paired with the sonar-ping keyframe animation.

motion.name: ease-snap
motion.purpose: The system's universal easing curve. Used for all transitions and entrance animations.

motion.name: reveal-up
motion.purpose: Scroll-triggered section reveal. Starts at opacity 0, translateY(28px). Applied by IntersectionObserver at threshold 0.18.

motion.name: hero-entrance
motion.purpose: Page-load entrance for hero elements. Starts at translateY(32px). rAF-triggered, not scroll-triggered.

motion.name: glass-hover
motion.value: background 0.4s ease, box-shadow 0.4s ease, border-color 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)
motion.purpose: Glass card state transition on hover: border gains red tint, card lifts 2px.

motion.name: stagger-d40
motion.purpose: Stagger delay class .d-40 — first item in a staggered entrance sequence.

motion.name: stagger-d520
motion.purpose: Stagger delay class .d-520 — for the redline SVG motif, which appears last.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Navigation redline disappears, bottom project grid collapses from 3 to 2 columns.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Shell padding drops to 22px, all grids collapse to single column, timeline switches to list layout.

components.name: Glass Card
components.description: Primary container. A surface with material properties — blur and saturation create depth; the tint is nearly invisible.
components.css: .ds-glass-card { position: relative; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.045); backdrop-filter: blur(22px) saturate(165%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(22px) saturate(165%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.22), inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.02), inset 0 -14px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.22), 0 18px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.45); border-radius: 18px; padding: 22px 24px; overflow: hidden; isolation: isolate; transition: background 0.4s ease, box-shadow 0.4s ease, border-color 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-glass-card:hover { border-color: rgba(226,56,56,0.45); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-glass-card-text { font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: #a3a3a3; margin: 0; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Navigation Pill
components.description: Glass pill that expands to show nav links. Closed state shown here.
components.css: .ds-nav-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0; padding: 10px 18px; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.045); backdrop-filter: blur(22px) saturate(165%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(22px) saturate(165%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.22), 0 18px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.45); transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-nav-pill:hover { transform: scale(1.04); } .ds-nav-icon { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; }

components.name: Section Label
components.description: The system's section-heading motif. The only place label typography and Ember Red combine as a design element.
components.css: .ds-section-label { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #e23838; margin-bottom: 28px; } .ds-section-label-bar { width: 26px; height: 2px; background: #e23838; transform: skewX(-24deg); box-shadow: 0 0 6px rgba(226,56,56,0.6); flex-shrink: 0; display: block; }

components.name: Filter Pill — Active
components.description: Active state of Eric's Corner category filter. Filled with Ember Red.
components.css: .ds-filter-pill-active { padding: 7px 18px; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; font-family: sans-serif; cursor: pointer; background: rgb(226,56,56); color: #fff; border: 1px solid rgb(226,56,56); transition: opacity 0.18s ease; } .ds-filter-pill-active:hover { opacity: 0.88; }

components.name: Filter Pill — Inactive
components.description: Inactive state of Eric's Corner category filter. Ghost bordered pill.
components.css: .ds-filter-pill-inactive { padding: 7px 18px; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; font-family: sans-serif; cursor: pointer; background: transparent; color: #a3a3a3; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); transition: color 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease; } .ds-filter-pill-inactive:hover { border-color: rgba(226,56,56,0.55); color: #f5f5f5; }

components.name: Inline Brand Chip
components.description: Glass pill for inline brand references in prose (employer, company). Sits mid-line via translateY.
components.html: Company
components.css: .ds-inline-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; transform: translateY(-2px); padding: 4px 13px 4px 9px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.07); backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(160%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(160%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.22), 0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); color: #f5f5f5; font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1; transition: border-color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-inline-chip:hover { border-color: rgba(226,56,56,0.50); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.22), 0 4px 18px rgba(226,56,56,0.20); } .ds-chip-logo { width: 27px; height: 18px; border-radius: 5px; background: #fff; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; overflow: hidden; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Flip Card — Front
components.description: Timeline entry card front face. Shows company logo, role, and date. Hover triggers a 3D CSS flip to reveal description.
components.html: CompanyRole TitleJan 2024 — Present
components.css: .ds-flip-wrap { width: 280px; height: 200px; border-radius: 18px; } .ds-flip-card-front { width: 100%; height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 5px; padding: 16px 20px; text-align: center; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.045); backdrop-filter: blur(22px) saturate(165%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.22), 0 18px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.45); border-radius: 18px; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-flip-card-logo { width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 8px; background: #fff; padding: 5px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-flip-card-info { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 3px; } .ds-flip-card-company { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #a3a3a3; font-family: sans-serif; } .ds-flip-card-role { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #f5f5f5; letter-spacing: -0.01em; font-family: sans-serif; } .ds-flip-card-date { font-size: 11px; color: #a3a3a3; letter-spacing: 0.04em; font-family: sans-serif; }

narrative.overview:
The site's terse structure is the dead serious part — the sparse hero, the dry one-liner, the timeline that states facts without commentary. The "still alive" is in the margins: ember particles that drift across the dark field, a navigation character that winks, an intro panel that admits "I also try to touch grass, but still working on it." The system never explains itself; it just lets those moments exist.

Depth is material, not metaphorical. Glass surfaces refract the drifting gradient field beneath them. A red hairline cuts through the hero at an angle, glowing faintly like heated metal. The embers are not decorative — they are the most literal expression of the system's warmth, given physical form. Components are liquid glass shells: the material itself is the surface. Animations are purposeful and tasteful; they respond to interaction without performing for attention.

The system rejects the template. No skills section. No hero metrics. No grid of rounded-icon features. One name, one honest sentence, and a set of carefully earned details that reward slower reading.
keyCharacteristics: Near-void dark field with three drifting gradient blobs shifting the ambient light
Liquid glass surfaces that blur (22px) and saturate (165%) the layer below
Ember Red (#e23838) as the sole primary accent — labels, highlights, active states, structural motifs
Hearth Amber (#e8a045) restricted to ambient warmth only: ember particles and ongoing-state indicators
Inter throughout — contrast through weight variation (300 to 600), not family contrast
Entrance animations: upward float on hero load, scroll-reveal on subsequent sections; never choreographed

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Ember Red appears on 15% or less of any given screen's visual area. Its rarity is what makes the redline motif, section labels, and active states register. Overuse cancels the system.

rules.name: The Amber Fence Rule
rules.body: Hearth Amber is ambient warmth only: ember particles and the ongoing-state sonar ping. It never appears as text, as a button background, or as a label. The moment amber becomes functional it loses its warmth.

rules.name: The Glass Tint Rule
rules.body: Glass surface transparency (rgba(255,255,255,0.045)) is intentionally near-invisible. The glass is the blur and saturation, not the white tint. Increasing the tint above 0.07 turns glass into a frosted block.

rules.name: The Weight-Contrast Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy comes from weight, not size alone. Weight 600 paired with weight 300 creates more contrast than mismatched families at smaller differences. Do not introduce a second typeface to solve a hierarchy problem weight can fix.

rules.name: The One Uppercase Rule
rules.body: The .section-label motif is the single place uppercase runs as a design element. One per section maximum. Cards, tooltips, and descriptions are never uppercase.

rules.name: The Glass-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: When a new elevated surface needs depth, blur first — shadow second. A drop-shadow without a corresponding glass surface is an orphaned cue. The blur IS the elevation.

rules.name: The State-Response Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are visually flat at rest. Shadows, glows, and border highlights appear only in response to state (hover, active, ongoing). A resting card and a hovered card are materially distinct.

dos: Do use the .glass class for all elevated surfaces. The class carries backdrop-filter, border, inset shadows, and the top-edge highlight as a calibrated unit.
Do use cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for all transitions and animations. This is the system's single easing curve.
Do use clamp() for all display, headline, title, and body-large text sizes. Fixed pixel values are prohibited for fluid-scale roles.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. Every animation must have a reduced-motion override: instant or crossfade.
Do limit Ember Red to 15% or less of any screen's visual surface.
Do apply text-wrap: balance to all heading elements and text-wrap: pretty to multi-line body prose.
Do animate transform and opacity only. backdrop-filter and border-color are permitted exceptions for glass state transitions.
donts: Don't ship the generic dev portfolio scaffold: avatar hero, skills-progress-bar grid, flat card timeline, three identical project tiles.
Don't design toward an agency-polished, over-formal aesthetic. The site should feel like Eric built it.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Emphasis is through weight or size, not color gradients.
Don't use Hearth Amber (#e8a045) as a text color, button accent, or functional indicator.
Don't put the .section-label motif on every card, tooltip, or inline content block. One per section, at the section opening.
Don't add a skills section, progress bars for abilities, or hero metric statistics.
Don't recreate glassmorphism inline. The .glass class values are calibrated together.
Don't introduce a second typeface. The system is Inter-only; weight contrast is the hierarchy.
Don't animate layout properties (width, height, padding).
Don't use Hearth Amber on interactive elements to signal urgency or importance — that is Ember Red's role.
